code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test10-OCT-2018Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine), with significant proteinuria and ketonuria, low urine specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). Blood glucose and HbA1c are low, suggesting no hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Findings are consistent with possible chronic kidney disease and protein-losing nephropathy; consider further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes.

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
